claims.claim: _handle_interruption does full teardown: text_aggregator.handle_interruption, clears frame sequencer, resets word timestamps, stops audio-context task, drains serialization queue keeping uninterruptible frames, calls on_audio_context_interrupted per context
claims.found: async def _handle_interruption(...): await self._text_aggregator.handle_interruption() ... self._aggregated_frame_sequencer.clear() ... await self.reset_word_timestamps() ... await self._stop_audio_context_task() ... self._serialization_queue.reset() ... for ctx_id in audio_contexts: await self.on_audio_context_interrupted(context_id=ctx_id) (lines 902-922)

claims.claim: Cartesia sends per-context cancel {"context_id": ctx, "cancel": true}
claims.found: async def on_audio_context_interrupted(...): cancel_msg = json.dumps({"context_id": context_id, "cancel": True}); await self._get_websocket().send(cancel_msg) (lines 609-614)

claims.claim: InterruptibleTTSService designed for TTS services that don't support word timestamps, reconnects websocket on interruption while speaking (basis for prior-art divergence mechanism 2)
claims.found: Docstring: 'Designed for TTS services that don't support word timestamps. Handles interruptions by reconnecting the websocket when the bot is speaking and gets interrupted.'

summary: All four code_refs and all three source figures verified against ground truth. Quotes are verbatim; line offsets are within 1-3 lines (PDF parse column-wrap). No WRONG or MISCITED claims.
